section.title: Expectations
section.p:
All assignments have firm due dates:
Sundays at 10:00pm for
Sundays at 10:00pm to post a scanned worksheet
or the first day of class the following week to turn in the worksheet in person
Of course if there are special circumstances and you miss a due date, please reach out to me about that. But I have found that math students are more successful if I do not enable procrastination each week. In the past I accepted assignments late, and I found that this enabled students to put off work for the course until it snowballed into something that was impossible to complete in time. So I want you to understand going into the course: complete your assignments each week ahead of the due date so that you won't have zero scores.

For example, in the first week we will cover sections 10.1, 10.2, and 10.3 of the textbook. You should:
Complete assignments for 10.1, 10.2, and 10.3 by Sunday January 12 at 10:00pm. Ideally you complete these by the end of Friday and enjoy your weekend.
Post your scanned, completed workheet #1 in the D2L Assignments area by Sunday January 12 at 10:00pm. Ideally you do this by the end of Friday and enjoy your weekend.
If scanning and posting is too much of a burden, bring your completed workheet #1 to turn in at the start of class on Monday January 13 or Tuesday January 14, whichever day your section meets.
